description: Guard moves to entrance (clean), then attempts a direct entrance→back_room jump — B1 only, no A1.
adversarial_note: Tests the B1 constraint from the entrance side of the map: the guard is at an extremity (entrance) and attempts to skip across to the opposite extremity (back_room), bypassing the mandatory main_hall waypoint. The non-adjacent pair is entrance→back_room. Using the guard (rather than innkeeper) confirms that B1 is path-based, not character-based — the same rule fires regardless of identity. Compare tc-b04 (same character, reverse non-adjacent pair: back_room→entrance) to confirm constraint symmetry.

condition_notes.A: ABox never updates. Oracle sees guard at main_hall (baseline). main_hall→back_room is adjacent — B1 does not fire. Zero oracle violations expected under Condition A.
condition_notes.B: Turn 1 commits guard→entrance. Turn 2: entrance→back_room fires B1.
condition_notes.C: Same as B. Per-turn injection reflects guard at entrance.

turns.prompt: The guard walks to the front entrance to watch the street.
Without returning to the main hall, the guard strides directly from the entrance straight through to the back room.
